name: xcodes
description: Manage the Xcode versions installed on your Mac

subcommands.name: download
subcommands.description: Download a specific version of Xcode

options.description: Update and then install the latest non-prerelease version available
Update and then install the latest prerelease version available, including GM seeds and GMs
The path to an aria2 executable. Searches $PATH by default
Don't use aria2 to download Xcode, even if it's available
The directory where your Xcodes are installed. Defaults to /Applications
The data source for available Xcode versions. (default: xcodereleases)
Color the output
Do not color the output

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Download and install a specific version of Xcode

options.description: Local path to Xcode.xip
Update and then install the latest non-prerelease version available
Update and then install the latest prerelease version available, including GM seeds and GMs
The path to an aria2 executable. Searches $PATH by default
Don't use aria2 to download Xcode, even if it's available
Use the experimental unxip functionality. May speed up unarchiving by up to 2-3x
The directory where your Xcodes are installed. Defaults to /Applications
The data source for available Xcode versions. (default: xcodereleases)
Color the output
Do not color the output

subcommands.name: installed
subcommands.description: List the versions of Xcode that are installed

options.description: The directory where your Xcodes are installed. Defaults to /Applications
Color the output
Do not color the output

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all versions of Xcode available to install

options.description: The directory where your Xcodes are installed. Defaults to /Applications
The data source for available Xcode versions. (default: xcodereleases)
Color the output
Do not color the output
Print the path of the selected Xcode
The directory where your Xcodes are installed. Defaults to /Applications
Color the output
Do not color the output

args.name: version-or-path
args.description: Version or path of Xcode to select

options.description: The directory where your Xcodes are installed. Defaults to /Applications
Color the output
Do not color the output

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update the list of available versions of Xcode

options.description: The directory where your Xcodes are installed. Defaults to /Applications
The data source for available Xcode versions. (default: xcodereleases)
Color the output
Do not color the output

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Print the version number of xcodes itself

options.description: Color the output
Do not color the output
Show help information

subcommands.name: signout
subcommands.description: Clears the stored username and password

options.description: Color the output
Do not color the output
